commit.message:
Clean up: remove duplicate migration 0013, add check-compat script

Migration was renamed 0013→0011; stage the deletion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

End-to-end deploy wizard, destroy UX, list command, doctor diagnostics (v0.1.10)

Wire OpenRouter key validation into deploy wizard so invalid keys are
caught before deployment. Add machine state check to destroy so stopped
machines get manual BotFather instructions instead of SSH errors. Add
fleet listing command with table truncation for long app names. Expand
doctor with volume, secrets, process, gateway, and API connectivity
checks. Harden messaging setup with token API validation and re-prompts.
Bridge all messaging and LLM secrets into .env on boot.

277 tests, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(daemon): Structured MappingFired event emission (#492)

* feat(daemon): Structured MappingFired event emission (#482)

Add MappingFiredPayload struct with trigger info, action summary, result,
latency, and mode context. Implement summarize_action() helper for
human-readable action descriptions. Emit mapping_fired events on the
monitor channel after action execution in process_device_event().

- MappingFiredPayload, FiredTriggerInfo, FiredActionInfo in event_types.rs
- summarize_action() covers all 15 Action variants
- action_type_string() for machine-readable action type
- extract_trigger_info() maps ProcessedEvent → trigger metadata
- Non-blocking emission via existing push_monitor_event()
- 22 unit tests for summarize_action + payload serialization

Closes #482

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Use UTF-8-safe string truncation in summarize_action()

Replace byte-index slicing (&text[..27]) with truncate_str() helper
that finds the nearest char boundary. Prevents panics on multi-byte
input (emoji, accented characters). Adds 2 multi-byte tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address PR review — matched event tracking, unused imports, tests

- Track which ProcessedEvent matched the rule (matched_event_idx) and
  pass only that event to extract_trigger_info(), fixing wrong trigger
  reported when multiple events exist (e.g., PadPressed before ChordDetected)
- Remove unused OscArg import from event_types.rs
- Remove unused VelocityMapping import from test_summarize_send_midi_cc
- Add 8 unit tests for extract_trigger_info covering note, gamepad,
  CC, chord, double tap, encoder, empty events, and matched-event isolation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Drop executor lock before payload emission, fix latency + mode fallback

- Scope executor mutex guard to just the execute() call, dropping it
  before payload serialization and handle_dispatch_result().await
- Start latency timer after acquiring the lock so it measures only
  action execution, not lock contention
- Use "unknown" fallback for mode_name instead of empty string,
  consistent with existing mapping_matched emission

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: Fix rustfmt formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Log serialization failures, use Value-based test assertions

- Replace silent if-let with match + warn! for MappingFiredPayload
  serialization failure (observable diagnostics)
- Replace substring-based JSON assertions with serde_json::Value
  path assertions (unambiguous, reorder-resilient)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Use end-to-end latency (event receipt → action completion)

Use processing_start (captured at event receipt) for mapping_fired
latency_us instead of executor-only timing. Matches ADR-014 spec
and existing latency monitor event semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Emit mapping_fired after dispatch, add FiredResult enum, structured payload (#493)

- Move mapping_fired emission after handle_dispatch_result() so stream
  ordering is consistent and result reflects final outcome
- Replace free-form result String with FiredResult enum (Ok|Error) for
  type safety
- Add payload: Option<serde_json::Value> field to MonitorEvent (#493)
- Emit structured payload in MonitorEvent.payload, human-readable
  summary in MonitorEvent.detail (no more JSON-in-JSON)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

feat: Insights tab on dashboard — call center intelligence widget panel

Toggle pill (AI Suggestions | ✦ Insights) above the cards area.

Insights panel: 6 seeded widgets per company profile —
  1. Call Volume 24h — big number + live dot + SVG sparkline + % delta
  2. CSAT Score 7-day — big number + 7-bar week chart + trend
  3. Trending Topics — 4-topic animated bar chart (full width)
  4. Top Performer — agent card with gradient avatar + CSAT badge
  5. Open Escalations — count + critical/medium/low breakdown
  6. Churn Risk — count + severity progress bar (full width)

All numbers deterministically seeded from companyConfig.companyName so
they feel real and are consistent across reloads. Full light/dark support
via design-system CSS tokens. Staggered entrance animations on every widget.

- Multi-gateway independence tests (7 new test cases)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(v3): Fix false-positive maths detection across all 198 topics

Comprehensive audit found 4 non-maths topics getting wrong arithmetic:
- "Summary Writing" (English) matched "sum" -> got addition numbers
- "Reproduction in Plants" (Science) matched "multi" -> got multiplication
- "Time (hour, half-hour)" matched "half" -> got fraction numbers
- "Factors and multiples" matched "multipl" -> got multiplication

Root cause: substring matching in _detect_maths_operation() was too greedy.

Fix:
- Added non-maths topic exclusion list (checks topic name first)
- Made keyword matching more precise (e.g. "sum" as whole word only)
- "half/quarter" excluded when topic contains "time/hour/clock"
- "factors/multiples/hcf/lcm" return None (conceptual, not arithmetic)
- All 28 edge-case tests pass, full 198-topic audit clean

commit.message:
Update MCP server icon URLs to use GitHub-hosted SVG logos (#1670)

## Summary
Updated the icon URLs for AWS and Azure MCP server configurations to use
SVG logos hosted on GitHub instead of Simple Icons CDN.

## Changes
- **AWS MCP Server**: Changed icon URL from
`https://cdn.simpleicons.org/amazonwebservices/232F3E` to
`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gilbarbara/logos/main/logos/aws.svg`
- **Azure MCP Server**: Changed icon URL from
`https://cdn.simpleicons.org/microsoftazure/0078D4` to
`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gilbarbara/logos/main/logos/microsoft-azure.svg`

## Implementation Details
Both icon URLs now point to the gilbarbara/logos GitHub repository,
which provides high-quality SVG versions of cloud provider logos. This
change improves icon consistency and reliability by using a dedicated
logo repository instead of relying on the Simple Icons CDN.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01UvUq5Lp1y8Nw8Y7Hmn6HHZ

images, ensuring more reliable display of provider icons in the UI.

commit.message:
fix: remove all model discovery — use LiteLLM "local" alias everywhere

- app.py: replace llm_model_id global + startup discovery with LLM_MODEL="local" constant;
  chat, agent/status endpoints use LLM_MODEL fallback
- agent/__init__.py: remove models.list() auto-discovery, use "local" directly
- product_classifier.py: remove models.list() auto-discovery, use "local" directly
- web_indexer.py: remove models.list() auto-discovery, use "local" directly

The "local" alias in LiteLLM always maps to the active vLLM backend (injected by
switch-model.sh). RAG service no longer decides which model to use.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix yandex_check_access using wrong company for multi-company users

get_user_company() returned the first membership (could be viewer),
while the UI shows a different selected company. Now uses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate to Convex backend and add chat-based generation experience

Phase 2: Convex Migration
- Replace Supabase/Drizzle with Convex for backend (schema, actions, queries)
- Replace OpenAI with Anthropic Claude via @convex-dev/agent
- Add Ticketmaster search, OG image scraping, and classifications tools
- Remove Spotify, Inngest, and all old API routes
- Add ConvexClientProvider and real-time subscriptions

Phase 3: Chat Experience
- New /generation page with real-time chat UI
- Three message types: thinking trail (gray timeline), question cards (blue),
  and trip cards (image + details on white space)
- Agent can ask follow-up questions via [ASK_USER] output format
- Reorder onboarding: interests → timeframe → music → generation
- Trip cards show brush stroke images, Fraunces titles, city/date chips,
  key events with emojis, and Explore CTA button
- Bundle detail back button returns to chat when navigating from chat
- Generation ID persisted in localStorage for session continuity

commit.message:
fix: remove A18 hash mapping — shared with A19

Testing revealed iPhone 16 Pro (A18) and iPhone 17 Pro (A19) produce
identical GPU hash 967330bc. Hash cannot disambiguate these chips.
Devices still get unique entries via localStorage device ID.

commit.message:
Fix crash when saving project editor changes

The editor window was being deallocated while still closing because
currentEditorWindow was set to nil in the onSave callback before
window?.close() completed. Now cleanup happens in windowWillClose
via onDismiss callback, ensuring the window fully closes first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix category switcher collapsed in sidebar (#25)

* Add action figure category support with sidebar switcher

Introduces a category system (Transformers / Action Figures) so collectors
of non-transforming figures (GI Joe, Star Wars, He-Man, etc.) get their
own filtered experience. Sidebar toggle switches between categories;
all views, leaderboards, market pulse, and stats filter by active category.

- DB migration adds category column to Figures table
- Category switcher in sidebar with collapsed abbreviations (TF/AF)
- Scale-based class tiers for action figures (3.75", 6", 7", 12")
- Opt-in "Has Transformation?" checkbox on submission/scorecard forms
  (default checked for Transformers, unchecked for Action Figures)
- Dynamic score formula: /90 with transformation, /70 without
- All 16 API endpoints filter by ?category= query param
- All frontend fetches pass active category

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump styles.css version to bust cache for category switcher

The category switcher CSS wasn't loading because styles.css cache
version wasn't bumped with the other files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix category switcher crushed by flex shrink in sidebar

The sidebar is a flex column and when content exceeded viewport height,
the category-switcher was shrinking to 1.8px due to default flex-shrink.
Adding flex-shrink: 0 prevents it from collapsing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add grabbed mode: gray background, no cursor when clicking blue bar

Clicking the blue grab handle enters "grabbed" mode — content area
turns gray, cursor is removed, contenteditable is disabled. This
signals non-edit mode for text, tables, and images. Double-click
the content area to re-enter edit mode. Delete key works in
grabbed mode.
